title: Lapasathus lanceolata

As part of the Rubiaceae family, Lapasathus lanceolata shares characteristics and traits with other plants within this family. The Rubiaceae family is further classified under the larger clade of asterids, a group of flowering plants. intro: Lapasathus lanceolata, also known as Thai jasmine, is a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Thailand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and inflammation, thanks to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lapasathus lanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lapasathus lanceolata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional Thai medicine, where it is valued for its therapeutic benefits.
